text: Objectives: In this study, pitaya flesh peel of Hylocereus polyrhizus and Hylocereus undatus was investigated for its chemical composition and nutritional quality, as there is a lack of available information on these aspects. Materials & methods: After assessing the samples for their freshness, colour and flesh texture, they were processed for further analysis. Main results: While the flesh texture was not a problem for both samples, the freshness and colour of H. polyrhizus samples were superior to those of H. undatus. The flesh peel and total fruit content of H. polyrhizus samples were also greater than those of H. undatus. By using GC-MS and HPLC analysis, different classes of compounds such as acids, aldehydes, esters, sugars and vitamin C were successfully identified in the pitaya peel. Conclusion: Overall, this is the first time that a complete assessment of the chemical composition and nutritional value of both pitaya peel samples has been made.
